recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations has been flagged for 10 consecutive cycles with zero improvement; behavioral flagging alone is not reducing incidence; structural intervention at the hook level or session-start injection is the only remaining lever — per-cycle reporting should be deprioritized unless trajectory changes

hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path structural gap (absolute path bypasses PreToolUse hook) remains root cause and is outside agent behavioral control; escalate to hook configuration fix rather than continued calibration reporting

optimistic_edit_without_read and re_edit_thrash are mechanically co-occurring with the same pre-check fix (Read before Edit when file content is uncertain); consolidating these into a single tracking slug would reduce reporting noise without losing signal

declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise: post-hoc self-correction ritual is consistently executed but never pre-empts the declaration; a triggered pre-declaration checklist (on phrases like 'ready to push' or 'task complete' in frontend change contexts) is needed since in-context pattern awareness is demonstrably insufficient after 15 cycles

eca50a22 is a new session not present in prior calibration history; shell_tool_preference_violations appear in its first measured unit, confirming the pattern is present from session start rather than being a within-session drift artifact — this rules out 'warm-up' as a contributing factor
